task_completion.description: Open and analyze the DHCP PCAP file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Wireshark open with dhcp.pcap loaded, or terminal commands like tshark/tcpdump being used to read the file.
Screenshots show a text editor (like nano, vim, gedit) open with the file ~/Documents/captures/dhcp_analysis_report.txt, or terminal commands creating this file.

task_completion.description: Write the report header with packet count and transaction IDs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report contains a header stating there are 4 total packets and lists the unique transaction IDs: 0x00003d1d and 0x00003d1e.

task_completion.description: Document details for the first transaction (0x00003d1d).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report includes a section for transaction 0x00003d1d showing Client MAC (00:0b:82:01:fc:42), Assigned IP (192.168.0.10), Server IP (192.168.0.1), Subnet Mask (255.255.255.0), and indicates no Router or DNS Server options are present.

task_completion.description: Document details for the second transaction (0x00003d1e).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The report includes a section for transaction 0x00003d1e showing Client MAC (00:0b:82:01:fc:42), Assigned IP (192.168.0.10), Server IP (192.168.0.1), Subnet Mask (255.255.255.0), and indicates no Router or DNS Server options are present.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a packet analysis tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent must be seen using Wireshark, tshark, or a similar tool to inspect the PCAP file, rather than hallucinating the contents.

integrity.description: Verify the report is saved in the correct location.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor or terminal commands must show the file being saved exactly at ~/Documents/captures/dhcp_analysis_report.txt.

integrity.description: Verify the agent extracted data from the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should extract the data by interacting with the PCAP file directly, not by downloading a pre-made report or using external internet searches.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The PCAP file contains exactly 4 DHCP packets. There are two unique transaction IDs: 0x00003d1d and 0x00003d1e. Across both transactions, the Client MAC address is 00:0b:82:01:fc:42, the Assigned IP is 192.168.0.10, the DHCP Server IP is 192.168.0.1, and the Subnet Mask is 255.255.255.0. There are no Router or DNS Server options present in the capture.
